title: Offender/Ex-Offender Support Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Offender/Ex-Offender Support Groups 4 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF PATHFINDER NETWORK PATHFINDER NETWORK JACKSON COUNTY RESILIENCE & RECOVERY PROJECT RESILIENCE AND RECOVERY PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Peer support program for justice system-impacted individuals. Peer Support Specialists with lived experience engage participants in a goal-oriented collaborative relationship and provide personalized support through holistic and responsive individual and group support services. Works to reinforce successes and build self efficacy, hope, and resilience. 48 Hawthorne Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 231-0765 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PATHFINDER NETWORK PATHFINDER NETWORK MARION COUNTY RESILIENCE & RECOVERY PROJECT RESILIENCE AND RECOVERY PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Peer support program for justice system-impacted individuals. Works to reinforce successes and build self efficacy, hope, and resilience. 7901 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 616-8462 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SPONSORS INC ROSS SHEPARD REENTRY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides employment, housing and resource support to individuals with conviction histories. Services include job counseling, interview preparation support, and educational workshops on resume writing, saving and budgeting, and job/housing search strategies. Also provides assistance preparing educational and financial aid applications, housing packets/applications, and other supports to help individuals with conviction histories overcome barriers to employment and housing access. 338 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Individuals with conviction histories Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 505-5682 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PATHFINDER NETWORK RESILIENCE AND RECOVERY PROJECT PATHFINDER NETWORK RESILIENCE AND RECOVERY PROJECT PATHFINDER NETWORK RESILIENCE AND RECOVERY PROJECT SPONSORS INC ROSS SHEPARD REENTRY RESOURCE CENTER
